title: Finding the hypotenuse from two legs
problem: A right-angled triangle has legs of length $6$ cm and $8$ cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse.

steps.instruction: Take the square root of both sides.

title: Length of a ladder leaning against a wall
problem: A ladder leans against a wall with its base $0.7$ m from the wall and reaching $2.4$ m up the wall. How long is the ladder?

steps.instruction: Identify the right-angled triangle. The ladder is the hypotenuse, the horizontal distance is one leg ($0.7$ m), and the vertical height is the other leg ($2.4$ m).

title: Diagonal of a square
problem: A square has a side length of $10$ m. Find the length of its diagonal, to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Recognise that the diagonal of a square divides it into two right-angled isosceles triangles. Each triangle has two legs equal to the side length of the square.
Take the square root. The result is a surd — simplify and then evaluate to 2 decimal places.
